MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-05-2025 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANTS SUSANNA BAIR AND MARILYN BAIR'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND ALL RELATED DATES. HAIG ARSEN HARRIS, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JUDITH MCAULAY, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. JUDGE FENG MADE A DISCLOSURE THAT JUDITH MCAULAY, ESQ. WAS AN ASSOCIATE IN THE KERN'S OFFICE WITH HIM BEFORE AND HAVE NOT SEEN HER IN 15 YEARS, BUT CAN BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. NO OBJECTION BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 4/7/25 IS CONTINUED TO 10/6/25 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL PRE-TRIAL AND DISCOVERY DEADLINES SHALL TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ANY FURTHER CONTINUANCES OF THE TRIAL DATE MUST BE BROUGHT BY NOTICED MOTION. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO LODGE COURTESY COPIES THEREFORE A FILING FEE OF $60.00 HAS TO BE PAID IN ROOM 103. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE

Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Neurological Examination And Neuropsychological Examination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Dalal Kaddoura
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. HAIG ARSEN HARRIS, Jr., Esq. of Law Offices of Scampini, Mortara & Harris (tel: 415-421-8556) for the Plaintiff.
Atty. DALAL KADDOURA, Esq. (via zoom videoconference) of Straus Meyers, LLP (tel: 619-595-6020) for the Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS SUSANNA BAIR, AND MARILYN BAIR's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Submit To A Neurological Examination And Neuropsychological Examination.

The court provides counsel with a modified tentative ruling. Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its modified tentative ruling with some modifications as follows:

Granted.

Counsel and their representatives shall neither interfere nor obstruct the examiner during the entire time of the examination.

All raw data of the plaintiff's neuropsychological tests shall be transmitted to plaintiff's neuropsychological experts designated by respective counsel.

Per protective order agreed upon by counsel, the raw data transmitted to plaintiff's neuropsychological expert shall be destroyed thereafter by plaintiff's neuropsychological expert and a declaration under penalty of perjury from the expert is to be provided stating the raw data has been destroyed.

All raw test data of plaintiff, transmitted by the examiner, shall neither be disclosed nor disseminated to any other party or individual without court order.

Plaintiff's counsel may audio record the entire neuropsychological examination, including all interviews.

The independent neuropsychological expert shall inform counsel of specific tests to be conducted at time of said examination.

The IME report of defense neuropsychological expert shall be transmitted to defense and plaintiff's counsel, their associates, experts designated by respective counsel, no later than 30 days after completion of the examination.

In addition to physical examination the neurologist who will be conducting the neurological IME may inquire to the following:
the history of plaintiff's injury which is the subject of the litigation; all subjective and objective findings, any prior or subsequent similar injuries; residual complaints; plans for any surgeries, present and future; all diagnostic tests conducted and to be conducted, and other questions relating to the subject injuries.

The neurologist conducting the neurological IME shall not conduct any mental examination.

The IME report of the neurologist shall be transmitted to defense and plaintiff's counsel, their associates, experts designated by counsel no later than 30 days after the completion of the examination.

Counsel for the plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order which conforms with the Court's ruling and provide to opposing counsel for approval as to form, prior to submitting it to the court.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: V. da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/SKF)
